description: Probe discovers 7 interactive objects with 1px effects each. Model clicks randomly — no sequence planning. All effects are tiny (1px), making it hard to distinguish meaningful from meaningless clicks. 0/7 levels across 3 attempts.

0.8B model finds all 7 interactive objects but cannot form action sequences to solve levels. The planning gap — not perception — is the binding constraint. Scene description (describe_scene()) adds spatial context but doesn't help when the model can't reason about object relationships or goal states.

All 7 objects produce ~1px changes. With such tiny visual feedback, the model cannot distinguish 'this click moved toward the goal' from 'this click did nothing useful'. Larger visual changes (like lp85's 293px) give stronger learning signal.
